name: Engineering Audit Protocol (PR-F2, handover F-005)

design_notes.nature_of_this_feature: Handover F-005. Author ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md: the reusable evidence-first engineering audit protocol. Per .agent/feature_list_005.json feature 035 and product_spec_005.md FR-04/FR-06, the audit MUST use the evidence hierarchy and the ten-state maturity model (Designed..Production Proven, per docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md's Maturity Model section) with finding confidence, MUST enforce 'no claim may be promoted beyond its evidence' (the NIP's literal rule), and MUST explicitly exclude commercial/market/GA readiness from generic engineering scoring (kept to the separate, deferred GA gate, ecosystem/08_ga_gate.md). This feature is protocol-authoring only: no schema (039 owns schema/engineering_finding.schema.json, and depends on THIS feature so it cannot yet be in flight -- verification entry 17 positively asserts it does not yet exist, structurally catching 039 running out of dependency order), no tooling, no capability routing or module-index prose beyond the single Module Navigation table-row flip this feature's own deliverable requires (ecosystem/README.md's '03' row, from feature 032's index; matching the exact pattern contract 034 used for its own '02' row flip).
design_notes.planned_document_structure: ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md uses the same house pattern as the shipped ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md and ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md (numbered '## N. Title' sections): '## 1. Overview' (evidence-first framing, cross-referencing Preflight and Baseline as the preceding lifecycle steps); '## 2. When to Run' (states the literal tokens 'Preflight verdict' and 'Baseline' -- the audit consumes both as required inputs and MUST NOT begin without a PASS or operator-approved PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS preflight and a captured baseline for the current execution -- realizing 'evidence-first audit flow (consumes preflight verdict + baseline)'); '## 3. Evidence Hierarchy' (states the literal lowercase phrase 'evidence hierarchy' in body prose, not merely in the heading casing, and a four-tier ranking -- 'Tier 1' Verified (deterministic, machine-checked), 'Tier 2' Reproduced, 'Tier 3' Observed, 'Tier 4' Asserted -- every finding must cite); '## 4. Maturity Model' (lists all ten NIP maturity levels verbatim -- Designed, Authored, Implemented, Unit Tested, Integrated, Rendered, Deployed, Exercised, Observable, Production Proven -- states the literal NIP sentence 'No claim may be promoted beyond its evidence' and names this the literal 'no-promotion-beyond-evidence rule', tying each maturity claim's ceiling to its cited evidence tier from Section 3); '## 5. Finding Confidence Levels' (three confidence levels -- Confirmed (Tier 1-backed), Probable (Tier 2-backed), Suspected (Tier 3-backed) -- and states a Tier-4-only finding MUST NOT exceed Suspected); '## 6. Commercial Readiness Exclusion' (states the literal token 'Commercial' and the literal phrase 'MUST NOT be embedded in generic engineering scoring', explicitly deferring commercial/GA readiness to ecosystem/08_ga_gate.md per product_spec_005.md Sec 2.3 -- realizing 'Commercial readiness is not embedded in generic engineering scoring' as an explicit, defined exclusion, not merely an omission); '## 7. Audit Artifact' (states the literal path conventions `.agent/audits/<audit-id>/findings.json` and `.agent/audits/<audit-id>/report.md` per the NIP's Artifact Locations section, and a forward prose reference to schema/engineering_finding.schema.json as not-yet-present, matching 01/02's precedent for forward-referencing their own not-yet-shipped schemas); '## 8. References' (points back to the NIP, ecosystem/README.md, 01_clean_state_preflight.md, 02_evidence_baseline.md, methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md, methodology/03_circuit_breaker.md). Frontmatter: id 'nizam-ecosystem-engineering-audit', status 'active', version '0.1.0' (first cut, matching 01/02's own 0.1.0), authoritative_source 'ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md'. This structure is committed to here because the anchored, section-scoped verification entries below reference these exact heading strings verbatim; the generator's implementation step MUST use them verbatim or the section-anchored verification commands will legitimately fail (a heading-text mismatch is a defect to fix, per Circuit Breaker attempt 1, not a scope violation). A companion one-line edit to ecosystem/README.md's Module Navigation table flips only the '| 03 | ... | Planned |' row to '| 03 | ... | Shipped |' -- the table itself and its Shipped/Planned convention were authored by feature 032 (and the '01'/'02' rows already flipped by 033/034); this feature edits exactly the one pre-existing '03' cell and nothing else in the file.
design_notes.verification_mechanism_dry_run_proof: Before proposing the checks below, the section-scoped primitive (tools/verify_lib.sh vlib_section_grep, the same anti-vacuity-approved primitive contracts 031/033/034 used) was dry-run against a full scratch GOOD fixture (built and destroyed under the session scratchpad, never committed, confirmed via `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` unchanged before/after) matching the planned structure verbatim, and seven scratch BAD variants each derived from the GOOD fixture with exactly one target token/phrase removed via `sed` (bad_sec2.md: 'Preflight verdict' stripped; bad_sec3.md: 'evidence hierarchy' phrase stripped; bad_sec4_level.md: the 'Production Proven' line deleted; bad_sec4_rule.md: 'no-promotion-beyond-evidence rule' phrase stripped; bad_sec5.md: the 'Suspected' line deleted; bad_sec6.md: the 'MUST NOT be embedded in generic engineering scoring' phrase stripped; bad_sec7.md: the report.md artifact-path line deleted). Result: every GOOD-fixture positive assertion below (Sec2 'Preflight verdict'/'Baseline'; Sec3 'evidence hierarchy'/'Tier 1'..'Tier 4'; Sec4 all ten maturity-level names, 'no-promotion-beyond-evidence rule', and the literal 'No claim may be promoted beyond its evidence' sentence; Sec5 'Confirmed'/'Probable'/'Suspected'; Sec6 'Commercial'/the literal MUST-NOT phrase; Sec7 both artifact paths) returned exit 0; each corresponding BAD-variant negative probe returned exit 1 -- proving each check is discriminating and non-vacuous, not merely satisfied by any plausible-looking document. The whole-file feature_list AT literal (maturity/evidence/confidence tokens), the frontmatter schema validation, and the house-pattern frontmatter-fields check were also dry-run against the GOOD scratch fixture and passed. The README one-line-flip check was dry-run per the explicit lesson from contract 034's round-0 rejection (a stray backslash-before-backtick inside single quotes never matches plain-backtick content): the flip pattern here uses plain backticks inside single quotes throughout (`'| 03 | `03_engineering_audit.md` | Audit | Shipped |'`, no escaping). Dry-run proof: against the REAL, unmodified ecosystem/README.md the check correctly FAILs (the 03 row still reads 'Planned', exit 1, as expected pre-implementation); a scratch AFTER copy produced by a `sed` substitution changing ONLY the 03 row's last cell (Planned -> Shipped, confirmed via `diff -u` against a scratch BEFORE copy showing exactly one changed line, all seven other Module Navigation rows byte-identical) makes the check PASS (exit 0); `git diff --no-index --numstat` between the two scratch copies reports exactly '1\t1' (one line changed), calibrating the numstat-scoped one-line-edit guard (verification entry 13, a distinct check from entry 12) correctly. All other (non-content) checks -- scope guard, .agent immutability guard, schema/tools non-goal exclusions, planning-doc non-goal, tools/skill.json+SKILL.md non-goal, ecosystem per-file non-goal loop, 01/02/CHANGELOG non-goal, dependency-order guard, validate.sh green -- were additionally dry-run directly against the REAL, live working tree (mid-dry-run, feature 038 concurrently completed its own implementation -- schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json, schema/README.md, NIZAM.json, and 6 tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_*.json all landed as tracked/untracked changes during this session) and all PASSed, proving the concurrency-tolerant design (pathspec excludes rather than blind whole-directory assertions) correctly tolerates 038's now-fully-landed legitimate surface without a false-positive scope-guard failure; only the content-anchored checks against the not-yet-created ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md itself, and the README one-line-flip check, are expected to FAIL now and PASS after implementation. SELF-CAUGHT DEFECT (found during this same dry-run pass, before proposal): the initial draft of verification entry 7 (Evidence Hierarchy) mixed an `&&`-chained first check with a subsequent `for` loop, terminated by a bare `;` before a trailing `echo PASS` -- e.g. `checkA && for tok in ...; do ...; done; echo PASS`. Because the `;` before `echo PASS` is a top-level statement separator (not part of the `&&`-list), `echo PASS` executed unconditionally as the script's final statement regardless of whether `checkA` or the loop failed. Running this draft command verbatim against the real (currently nonexistent) ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md returned exit 0 -- a vacuous PASS on a file that does not exist, caught live by this same dry-run pass. Fixed by restructuring entry 7 into purely sequential statements, each gated by its own `|| { echo ...; exit 1; }` fallback (matching the safe pattern already used correctly in entries 8 and 9), so `echo PASS` is reached only if every prior check succeeded. Re-run after the fix: against the real, unmodified tree the corrected entry 7 now correctly FAILs (exit 1, 'Evidence Hierarchy section missing the evidence hierarchy phrase'); against the scratch GOOD fixture it PASSes; against the scratch bad_sec3.md (evidence-hierarchy phrase stripped) it correctly FAILs. All other verification entries were independently re-checked for this same `&&`-then-bare-`;`-then-unconditional-tail-echo anti-pattern and none exhibit it (entries 6, 10, 11 are single unbroken `&&...&&echo PASS || {...; exit 1;}` chains with no intervening `;`; entries 8, 9, and the corrected 7 use sequential `|| exit 1` gated statements throughout, with the final `echo PASS` reachable only after every prior statement's exit-on-failure guard has been passed).
design_notes.concurrency_fence: Feature 038 (Preflight Verdict Schema + Fixtures) is running CONCURRENTLY in this same working tree and, as observed live during this contract's own dry-run session, has now fully landed its authorized surface: NIZAM.json (modified: indexes schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json in both the top-level 'schemas' array and the 'schema' module's key_documents), schema/README.md (modified: new schema-table row + version bump), schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json (new), and six tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_*.json fixtures (new) -- plus its own .agent/contracts/038.json, .agent/qa/038*.json, and .agent/evidence/038-verify-*.txt artifacts. None of these are this feature's scope. This feature's scope-guard (verification entry 15) allow-lists NIZAM.json, schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json, schema/README.md, and the tools/fixtures/ prefix SOLELY to tolerate 038's concurrent legitimate edits without a false-positive scope-guard failure -- exactly as contract 034 allow-listed feature 038's earlier-in-flight surface (034's allow-list predates 038 declaring a NIZAM.json edit in its own scope; this contract's allow-list is written against 038's now-observed, complete surface, which does include NIZAM.json). Feature 036 (Progress Comparison Protocol, ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md) is contract-drafting concurrently: only .agent/contracts/036.json (and, as its own review proceeds, .agent/qa/036*.json artifacts) may appear during this feature's window; 036 does NOT touch ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md itself, because the orchestrator SERIALIZES README-flipping implementations across the sibling PR-F2 features that share ecosystem/README.md's Module Navigation table (034 flipped '02' before 038 began; this feature flips '03' before 036 is authorized to implement and flip '07') -- so 036 will not implement (and will not touch ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md or the README's '07' row) until after this feature's README edit has landed. This is why verification entry 20's per-file non-goal loop safely asserts ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md does not yet exist, and why this feature's README one-line-flip/diff-guard checks (entries 12-13, scoped to exactly one row) are not at risk of colliding with 036's eventual, later, disjoint row edit. Feature 037 (Baseline Schema + Fixtures, schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json) and feature 039 (Engineering Finding Schema + Fixtures, schema/engineering_finding.schema.json, which depends on THIS feature) are both NOT tolerated here: 039 cannot legitimately be in flight before 035 is complete (verification entry 17 positively asserts its schema file does not yet exist, structurally catching any out-of-dependency-order execution), and 037's declared surface (schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json) is excluded from the tolerated 038-scoped pathspec in verification entry 18, so an out-of-order 037 landing would correctly fail that check too.
design_notes.run_state_and_immutable_artifacts: This is a CONTRACT-PROPOSAL step (status=proposed): no implementation edits, no git commit, and no .agent/run_state.json rewrite happen under this document -- run_state.json is orchestrator-owned and batched centrally. Verification entry 16 (the .agent/qa + .agent/contracts immutability guard) mirrors the precedent in contracts 025/028/031/033/034 exactly: it excludes only this feature's own new 035.json contract and 035*.json qa/review artifacts, plus feature 038's own concurrently-authored 038.json contract and 038*.json qa/review artifacts, plus feature 036's own concurrently-drafted 036.json contract and 036*.json qa/review artifacts (038 and 036 are different, independently-scoped in-flight features, not historical/immutable ones -- same reasoning contract 034 applied to feature 038 before it had produced review artifacts). It deliberately does NOT check .agent/run_state.json, .agent/evidence/ (038's 26 evidence files are untracked but outside .agent/qa and .agent/contracts, so this guard never sees them), or any feature_list_00N.json (the orchestrator's own routinely-and-legitimately-modified concern).

files_create.purpose: The reusable, evidence-first engineering audit protocol: consumes the current execution's Preflight verdict and Baseline; defines a four-tier evidence hierarchy; assesses claims against the ten-state maturity model (Designed..Production Proven) under the explicit no-promotion-beyond-evidence rule; defines finding confidence levels (Confirmed/Probable/Suspected); states the machine-readable audit artifact convention `.agent/audits/<audit-id>/findings.json` + `.agent/audits/<audit-id>/report.md`; and explicitly excludes commercial/market/GA readiness from generic engineering scoring. UDS frontmatter per the house pattern (see design_notes.planned_document_structure).

files_modify.purpose: One-line Module Navigation table update: flip the pre-existing '03' row (`03_engineering_audit.md` / Audit) from 'Planned' to 'Shipped' now that this feature lands the file. No other edit to this file (table structure, other rows -- including 038's unrelated concurrent non-edits to this file -- and all other prose are feature 032's authored content and are left byte-for-byte intact except this one cell).

non_goals: No creation or edit of schema/engineering_finding.schema.json or any other schema/*.json file except the tolerated concurrent feature 038 surfaces (schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json, schema/README.md) -- the machine-readable finding schema is feature 039's scope (039 depends on this feature and cannot be in flight concurrently; verification entry 17 positively asserts the finding schema file does not yet exist)

No creation or edit of schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json (feature 037's scope, a sibling of this feature under the same 034 dependency, not tolerated here since 037 declares no NIZAM.json/schema/README.md/tools/fixtures surface this feature needs to accommodate)

No creation or edit of any file under tools/ except tolerating feature 038's concurrent tools/fixtures/* additions -- deterministic tooling is features 041/042's scope; this feature authors protocol prose only
No edit to tools/skill.json or tools/SKILL.md -- capability routing is feature 040's scope

No edit to NIZAM.json beyond tolerating feature 038's concurrent schema-index edit -- ecosystem capability registration is feature 040's scope; this feature does not add itself (or anything else) to NIZAM.json

No creation or edit of any other ecosystem/*.md protocol document (00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md, 04_dependency_reconciliation.md, 05_release_train_coordination.md, 06_simplification_review.md, 07_progress_comparison.md, 08_ga_gate.md) -- 07_progress_comparison.md is feature 036's scope (contract-drafting concurrently, not yet implementing per the concurrency_fence serialization note); the rest are deferred per product_spec_005.md Sec 2.3

No edit to ecosystem/README.md beyond the single '03' row Shipped/Planned cell -- the table structure, the Canonical Lifecycle prose, the Capability Routing section, the Consumer Convention section, and the already-flipped '01'/'02' rows are prior features' authored content and stay byte-for-byte intact

No edit to ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md or ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md (features 033/034's shipped deliverables) or CHANGELOG.md -- CHANGELOG consolidation for phase 005 is the release feature's (045, H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE) responsibility
No edit to docs/planning/manifest.json, docs/planning/ROADMAP.md, or docs/planning/DEBT.md -- no planning-artifact or debt changes are authorized by this feature

No edit to .agent/run_state.json, any .agent/contracts/*.json other than this 035.json, any .agent/qa/*.json, or any feature_list_00N.json -- those are immutable audit records or owned by the orchestrator, except tolerating feature 038's own in-flight 038.json/038*.json and feature 036's own in-flight 036.json/036*.json artifacts (neither historical nor this feature's concern)
No creation, edit, or deletion of the pre-existing untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md
No git commit of any kind is performed under this contract or its implementation step -- the deliverable is verified in the working tree; committing is a separate, later pipeline action
No execution of any outstanding phase-005 human gate (H-FRAMEWORK-SCOPE, H-DOGFOOD-EXCEPTION, H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE, H-RISK) -- this feature's operator_gate is false

No embedding of commercial, market, pricing, go-to-market, or GA-readiness criteria into this protocol's engineering scoring mechanics (Sections 3-5) -- that scoring is exclusively engineering maturity/evidence/confidence; commercial/GA readiness is explicitly named out of scope in Section 6 and deferred to the separate, deferred ecosystem/08_ga_gate.md

verification.acceptance_test: F-005/AT: the protocol doc file exists.
verification.command: test -f ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md && echo PASS || { echo "ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md missing"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (anti-vacuity master proof, mirroring the 031/033/034 fixture-probe precedent): ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md does not exist at HEAD, so every content-anchored check below (entries 3-11) is proven non-vacuous by construction -- nothing at HEAD can satisfy them because the target artifact itself does not yet exist there.
verification.command: ! git show HEAD:ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo PASS || { echo "ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md already exists at HEAD -- content checks below would be vacuous"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-005/AT: frontmatter validates against schema/frontmatter.schema.json.
verification.command: python3 -c "import json,yaml,jsonschema; d=open('ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; jsonschema.validate(yaml.safe_load(d), json.load(open('schema/frontmatter.schema.json')))" && echo PASS || { echo "frontmatter invalid"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (house-pattern frontmatter fields): id is 'nizam-ecosystem-engineering-audit', status is 'active', and authoritative_source is self-referential.
verification.command: python3 -c "import yaml,sys; d=open('ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; fm=yaml.safe_load(d); ok=(fm.get('id')=='nizam-ecosystem-engineering-audit' and fm.get('status')=='active' and fm.get('authoritative_source')=='ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md'); sys.exit(0 if ok else 1)" && echo PASS || { echo "frontmatter house-pattern fields incorrect"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-005/AT 'The protocol doc references the maturity model and the no-claim-beyond-evidence rule' (whole-file literal, verbatim from feature_list_005.json feature 035): the file names 'maturity', 'evidence', and 'confidence' (case-insensitive).
verification.command: python3 -c "t=open('ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md',encoding='utf-8').read().lower(); import sys; sys.exit(0 if ('maturity' in t and 'evidence' in t and 'confidence' in t) else 1)" && echo PASS || { echo "whole-file maturity/evidence/confidence token check failed"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-005/AT 'Audit uses evidence hierarchy and maturity model' (STRENGTHENED, section-anchored per the anti-vacuity mandate): the '## 2. When to Run' section states 'Preflight verdict' and 'Baseline' -- the audit's evidence-first flow consumes both.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_section_grep ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md '^## 2\. When to Run$' 'Preflight verdict' && vlib_section_grep ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md '^## 2\. When to Run$' 'Baseline' && echo PASS || { echo "When to Run section missing 'Preflight verdict' or 'Baseline'"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-005/AT 'Audit uses evidence hierarchy ... ' (STRENGTHENED, section-anchored): the '## 3. Evidence Hierarchy' section states the literal lowercase phrase 'evidence hierarchy' in body prose and all four evidence tiers (Tier 1-4).
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_section_grep ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md '^## 3\. Evidence Hierarchy$' 'evidence hierarchy' || { echo "Evidence Hierarchy section missing the 'evidence hierarchy' phrase"; exit 1; }; for tok in 'Tier 1' 'Tier 2' 'Tier 3' 'Tier 4'; do vlib_section_grep ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md '^## 3\. Evidence Hierarchy$' "$tok" || { echo "Evidence Hierarchy section missing tier token: $tok"; exit 1; }; done; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: F-005/AT '... and maturity model' (STRENGTHENED, section-anchored): the '## 4. Maturity Model' section names all ten NIP maturity levels verbatim and the literal no-promotion-beyond-evidence rule.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; for tok in Designed Authored Implemented 'Unit Tested' Integrated Rendered Deployed Exercised Observable 'Production Proven'; do vlib_section_grep ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md '^## 4\. Maturity Model$' "$tok" || { echo "Maturity Model section missing level: $tok"; exit 1; }; done; vlib_section_grep ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md '^## 4\. Maturity Model$' 'No claim may be promoted beyond its evidence' && vlib_section_grep ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md '^## 4\. Maturity Model$' 'no-promotion-beyond-evidence rule' && echo PASS || { echo "Maturity Model section missing the no-claim-beyond-evidence sentence or the named rule"; exit 1; }

Commercial Readiness Exclusion$' 'Commercial' && vlib_section_grep ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md '^## 6\. Commercial Readiness Exclusion$' 'MUST NOT be embedded in generic engineering scoring' && echo PASS || { echo "Commercial Readiness Exclusion section missing 'Commercial' or the literal MUST-NOT phrase"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (machine-readable audit artifact convention, per the NIP's 'Artifact Locations' section): the '## 7. Audit Artifact' section states the literal path conventions `.agent/audits/<audit-id>/findings.json` and `.agent/audits/<audit-id>/report.md`.

Audit Artifact$' '\.agent/audits/<audit-id>/report\.md' && echo PASS || { echo "Audit Artifact section missing the findings.json or report.md convention"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (README one-line flip, scoped to exactly the '03' row, plain-backtick pattern per the contract-034 round-0 lesson): ecosystem/README.md's Module Navigation table now marks the 03 row 'Shipped'.
verification.command: grep -Fq '| 03 | `03_engineering_audit.md` | Audit | Shipped |' ecosystem/README.md && echo PASS || { echo "ecosystem/README.md 03 row not flipped to Shipped"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (README one-line-only edit guard, dry-run proven against before/after scratch copies): ecosystem/README.md's diff against HEAD is exactly one inserted and one deleted line.
verification.command: out=$(git diff --numstat HEAD -- ecosystem/README.md); [ "$out" = "$(printf '1\t1\tecosystem/README.md')" ] && echo PASS || { echo "ecosystem/README.md diff is not exactly 1 insertion + 1 deletion: [$out]"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-005/AT: compliance validator stays green.
verification.command: bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed' && echo PASS || { echo "validate.sh SUMMARY regressed from 11 passed, 0 failed"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (scope guard, untracked-aware, concurrency-tolerant per design_notes.concurrency_fence -- NIZAM.json, schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json, schema/README.md, and tools/fixtures/ are feature 038's now-fully-landed concurrent scope, and v0.6.0-release-notes.md is a pre-existing untracked artifact this feature does not touch): only this feature's own new/modified files, plus the tolerated concurrent/pre-existing paths, changed outside .agent/.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_scope_guard 'ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md' 'ecosystem/README.md' 'schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json' 'schema/README.md' 'tools/fixtures/' 'NIZAM.json' 'v0.6.0-release-notes.md' && echo PASS || { echo "out-of-scope change(s) detected"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (.agent/ immutability guard, mirroring the precedent in contracts 025/028/031/033/034, extended per design_notes.concurrency_fence to tolerate feature 038's and feature 036's concurrently in-flight contract/qa artifacts): no historical (pre-existing, already-approved) .agent/qa or .agent/contracts artifact was edited -- excludes only this feature's own new 035.json contract and 035*.json qa/review artifacts, feature 038's own 038.json/038*.json artifacts, and feature 036's own 036.json/036*.json artifacts.
verification.command: changed=$(git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts ':(exclude).agent/contracts/035.json' ':(exclude,glob).agent/qa/035*.json' ':(exclude).agent/contracts/038.json' ':(exclude,glob).agent/qa/038*.json' ':(exclude).agent/contracts/036.json' ':(exclude,glob).agent/qa/036*.json'); if [ -n "$changed" ]; then echo 'immutable audit record(s) touched:'; echo "$changed"; exit 1; fi; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement, dependency-order guard per design_notes.concurrency_fence): schema/engineering_finding.schema.json (feature 039's deliverable, which depends on THIS feature) does not yet exist -- this feature performs zero schema work of its own, and this also structurally catches 039 running out of dependency order.
verification.command: test -f schema/engineering_finding.schema.json && { echo "schema/engineering_finding.schema.json already exists -- out of dependency order or out of this feature's scope"; exit 1; } || echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement, concurrency-tolerant via git pathspec excludes rather than a blind whole-directory assertion, per design_notes.concurrency_fence): every schema/*.json file except feature 038's declared schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json and schema/README.md, and every file under tools/ except feature 038's declared tools/fixtures/, are byte-identical to HEAD (this positively excludes feature 037's schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json from tolerance -- an out-of-order 037 landing would correctly fail this check).
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- schema/ tools/ ':(exclude)schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json' ':(exclude)schema/README.md' ':(exclude)tools/fixtures' && echo PASS || { echo "an out-of-scope schema/tooling file was modified"; git diff --name-only HEAD -- schema/ tools/ ':(exclude)schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json' ':(exclude)schema/README.md' ':(exclude)tools/fixtures'; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement): tools/skill.json and tools/SKILL.md are byte-identical to HEAD -- capability routing is feature 040's scope.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- tools/skill.json tools/SKILL.md && echo PASS || { echo "an out-of-scope capability-routing file was modified"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement, per-file existence loop per contract 033 rev1's ls-false-pass fix, reused correctly per contract 034's precedent): no other ecosystem/*.md protocol document (00, 04-08) was created.
verification.command: bad=0; for f in ecosystem/00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md ecosystem/04_dependency_reconciliation.md ecosystem/05_release_train_coordination.md ecosystem/06_simplification_review.md ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md ecosystem/08_ga_gate.md; do if [ -e "$f" ]; then echo "out-of-scope ecosystem protocol document created: $f"; bad=1; fi; done; if [ "$bad" -eq 0 ]; then echo PASS; else exit 1; fi

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement): ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md, ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md (features 033/034's shipped deliverables) and CHANGELOG.md are byte-identical to HEAD.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md CHANGELOG.md && echo PASS || { echo "an out-of-scope file (01_clean_state_preflight.md, 02_evidence_baseline.md, or CHANGELOG.md) was modified"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement): docs/planning/manifest.json, docs/planning/ROADMAP.md, and docs/planning/DEBT.md are byte-identical to HEAD.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- docs/planning/manifest.json docs/planning/ROADMAP.md docs/planning/DEBT.md && echo PASS || { echo "an out-of-scope planning document was modified"; exit 1; }

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/035-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (e.g. .agent/evidence/035-verify-01.txt for the first entry, ..., .agent/evidence/035-verify-22.txt for the twenty-second -- the 'verification' array has exactly 22 entries). Every evidence file MUST comply with methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5's Evidence Capture Convention: the exact invocation as the FIRST line, captured output next, a literal EXIT:<code> as the LAST line, replayable from the repository root, with NO session-absolute paths. One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.
